persona: first-time user of this new framework; has used similar product registries before; never used this one

findings.title: HTTP 400 leaks known task names
findings.expected: Generic 400 with a stable error code
findings.actual: Body lists every known task name
findings.suggestedFix: Add a stable error code ('unknown_task') and drop the known-tasks list from the body

findings.title: NaN weights propagate to the response
findings.repro: Pass quality_weight=float('nan') to score(...) and call the endpoint
findings.actual: NaN propagates and is serialized as NaN (invalid JSON)
findings.suggestedFix: Reject non-finite weights at the registry load time

findings.title: 'Benchmark weights are EDUCATED ESTIMATES' disclaimer is only in _comment
findings.expected: README-level caveat that applies to the in-tree benchmarks
findings.actual: _comment field only; README does not cross-reference it
findings.suggestedFix: Add a one-line cross-reference in the worktree README

findings.title: task_registry.py does not validate weight sum
findings.repro: Pass weights that sum to 2.0; call score(...)
findings.actual: Score is silently computed with bad weights
findings.suggestedFix: Add a validate_weights option that raises if weights are not in [0.0, 1.0] and sum to 1.0

findings.title: README claims 'responsive, modern' but no benchmark numbers are shown
Pydantic v1 style @validator is deprecated
findings.repro: from pydantic import validator triggers PydanticDeprecatedSince20

findings.title: Tests do not assert against the in-tree demo
findings.repro: Run python -m utils.auto_router.demo.run and compare to the assertions in the test files
findings.actual: The test files do not import or call the demo
findings.suggestedFix: Add a smoke test that runs the demo and asserts the documented pick
